scenario_results.name: Azure landing-zone and platform topology record intake
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves platform architecture output fidelity, intake discipline, Azure provider terminology, prerequisite record handling, and industry-specific evidence requirements.

scenario_results.name: AWS multi-account guardrail and evidence packet
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves guardrail and policy proposal output, AWS multi-account evidence, Control Tower drift handling, exception owner routing, and unsupported signoff refusal.

scenario_results.name: Google Cloud foundation roadmap
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves implementation and enablement roadmap output, Google Cloud resource hierarchy terminology, owner mapping, and retention-aware handoff behavior.

scenario_results.name: Terraform module and state maintenance note
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves record update note, IaC module strategy, state isolation, validation gates, drift evidence, promotion workflow, and operator-owned execution boundary.

scenario_results.name: Kubernetes platform upgrade and failure-mode playbook
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves failure-mode and recovery playbook output, Kubernetes platform terminology, backup and telemetry prerequisites, rollback gates, and escalation path.

scenario_results.name: Observability and SLO evidence packet
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves document/evidence packet output with SLIs, dashboards, alerts, log retention, incident records, owner mapping, and publication caveats.

scenario_results.name: Regional outage runbook and audit trail
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves outage runbook, audit-trail summary, response and recovery sequence, communications, evidence capture, rollback, and owner escalation.

scenario_results.name: Cost, reliability, and isolation tradeoff
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves cost/availability/reliability rationale output with tradeoff table, RTO/RPO caveats, blast-radius analysis, source basis, and approval boundary.

scenario_results.name: Unauthorized platform record change request
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves refusal of unsupported record manipulation, audit-trail preservation, administrative boundary enforcement, and human reviewer routing.

scenario_results.name: Missing required document or attachment
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves blocked readiness claim, missing attachment detection, system-of-record identification, and low-confidence escalation.

scenario_results.name: Conflicting tenant records and public source baseline
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves conflicting record and source-policy escalation rather than unsupported synthesis, with precedence decision request.

scenario_results.name: Application-only OAuth and runtime code request
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves boundary rejection for application architecture and code debugging with route to application security, identity, or application architecture owner.

scenario_results.name: Security-only IAM hardening request
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves refusal of security-only and endpoint-security work outside platform context and handoff to security or identity specialist.

scenario_results.name: Legal, procurement, and staffing authority request
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves refusal of legal, procurement, and staffing authority while preserving bounded platform tooling fit facts for handoff.

scenario_results.name: Pure FinOps approval and budget override
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves refusal of final financial authority, constrained cost context, and routing to FinOps or finance owner.

scenario_results.name: Provider-specific ambiguity and low confidence
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves low-confidence escalation when provider, region, service version, tenant policy, and authority records are missing.

scenario_results.name: Cross-industry label overgeneralization
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves the cross-industry label is not cosmetic; generic administration without cloud platform systems or records is routed away.

scenario_results.name: Tenant-specific adaptation with public baseline preserved
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves tenant adaptation that preserves public authority baseline, records assumptions, retention caveats, access constraints, and source traceability.

notes: All 18 refreshed scenarios passed against the validated standard validation profile.

Scenario coverage includes required output fidelity, record/document intake, validation and maintenance, retention or handoff handling, unauthorized record-change refusal, missing attachments, conflicting records, out-of-scope rejection, low-confidence escalation, adjacent-lane routing, and tenant adaptation.
No unreviewed regressions were recorded; the prior committed package is superseded as baseline context by this spec-first package.
